Partner / family visa — for partners of Dutch or EU residents

heroImageAlt: A British professional at a desk by a window overlooking a Dutch canal, focused on relocation planning. On the desk are a laptop showing a world map with a route to the Netherlands, an open notebook, a UK passport, documents labeled for Flights and planning, and a suitcase. In the background, Dutch canal houses, a bridge, and bicycles in soft daylight.

shipping.intro: Shipping from the United Kingdom typically takes 1–3 weeks depending on method. Many UK movers initially rent temporary housing before securing long-term accommodation.
notes: Common options: luggage-only move, partial household shipment, or full relocation container.
Short distance allows more flexible staging of move and travel.

seo.title: Moving to the Netherlands from the UK | Visa, Costs & Relocation Guide
seo.description: A practical relocation guide for UK citizens moving to the Netherlands including visa routes, relocation costs, documents, and planning tools.

faqOverrides.q: Do UK citizens need a visa to move to the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: Yes, for stays longer than 90 days. After Brexit, UK citizens follow non-EU rules and need a residence permit. Common routes include the Highly Skilled Migrant visa, EU Blue Card, partner visa, or entrepreneur visa.

faqOverrides.q: How long can UK citizens stay in the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: Without a visa, up to 90 days in 180 days (Schengen). For longer stays you need a residence permit via work, family, study, or entrepreneurship.

faqOverrides.q: How much does relocation from the UK cost?
faqOverrides.a: Costs vary by housing and method. Typical ranges include flights (€80–€250), shipping (€1,200–€4,000), temporary housing (€1,500–€3,000/month), and permit and setup costs. Use the relocation cost estimator for a personalised range.

faqOverrides.q: Can UK citizens work in the Netherlands after Brexit?
faqOverrides.a: Yes, with a valid residence permit that allows work, such as the Highly Skilled Migrant visa or EU Blue Card. Employers must be recognised sponsors for the HSM route.

faqOverrides.q: What documents are required when moving from the UK?
faqOverrides.a: Typically passport, birth and marriage certificates if relevant, employment contract, proof of address, qualifications, and health insurance proof. Some documents may need apostille certification.

scenarioOverrides.title: Finance professional relocating from London
scenarioOverrides.summary: Focus on financial sector roles, Amsterdam base, and Highly Skilled Migrant visa. Plan document prep, housing search, and first-week registration.

scenarioOverrides.title: Consultant relocating for EU market access
scenarioOverrides.summary: Focus on multinational companies and business relocation. Coordinate visa, housing, and registration with employer support.

scenarioOverrides.title: Entrepreneur launching EU business
scenarioOverrides.summary: Focus on startup relocation and Dutch business registration. Plan for entrepreneur visa, bank account, and company setup.

scenarioOverrides.title: Family relocation from the UK
scenarioOverrides.summary: Focus on schools, housing, and family visa routes. Plan for longer lead time on documents and housing that fits family size.

customNotes: UK movers often compare international transfers, temporary housing, and shipping options for UK–EU moves.
